notes: commit=abb2732ad0d759d750c2e38b616f7f3e949ed2f9; extraction_method=ast; doc_density_composite=80.0; params are name-only placeholders (annotation='Any') -- EXP-1 classifier tests selection only, not call-correctness.

tools.name: idb_open
tools.docstring: Open a binary, activate it, and warm up subsystems in one call.

tools.name: idb_list
tools.docstring: List open IDA sessions.

tools.name: decompile
tools.docstring: Decompile function(s) at address(es); returns pseudocode and per-item errors.

tools.name: disasm
tools.docstring: Disassemble function with offset/max_instructions pagination and optional total count.

tools.name: func_profile
tools.docstring: Profile functions with summary metrics and optional sampled details.

tools.name: analyze_batch
tools.docstring: Run comprehensive analysis over one or more target functions.

tools.name: xrefs_to
tools.docstring: Return xrefs to address(es) or named symbols, capped per target with truncation flag.

tools.name: xref_query
tools.docstring: Query xrefs with direction/type filters and pagination.

tools.name: xrefs_to_field
tools.docstring: Get cross-references to structure fields

tools.name: callees
tools.docstring: Return unique callees per function, capped by limit.

tools.name: find_bytes
tools.docstring: Search byte patterns (supports ??) with offset/limit pagination.

tools.name: basic_blocks
tools.docstring: Return function CFG blocks with offset/max_blocks pagination.

tools.name: find
tools.docstring: Search strings/immediates/refs for targets with offset/limit pagination.

tools.name: insn_query
tools.docstring: Query instructions with mnemonic/operand filters and scoped scans.

tools.name: export_funcs
tools.docstring: Export function data for addresses in json/c_header/prototypes formats.

tools.name: callgraph
tools.docstring: Build bounded callgraph from roots with depth/node/edge limits.

tools.name: analyze_function
tools.docstring: Compact single-function analysis: pseudocode, strings, constants, callers, callees, xrefs, blocks.

tools.name: analyze_component
tools.docstring: Analyze related functions as a group: per-function summaries, internal call graph, shared data.

tools.name: diff_before_after
tools.docstring:
Rename a function, set its type, or add a comment, and immediately see the
before/after decompilation side by side. Use this instead of calling rename
then decompile separately when you want to verify that a rename or type change
actually improved readability. Actions: 'rename_func' (action_args: {name: str}),
'set_type' (action_args: {type: str}), 'set_comment' (action_args: {comment: str}).
Returns {before, after, action_applied, changes_detected}. Especially useful
during batch renaming to confirm each change had the intended effect.

tools.name: trace_data_flow
tools.docstring:
Follow cross-references from or to an address, automatically traversing
multiple hops. Use 'forward' to see where data flows TO (xrefs-from), or
'backward' to see where data flows FROM (xrefs-to). At each node in the
traversal, returns the function name, instruction, and whether it's code or
data. Use this when you find an interesting string, constant, or global and
want to understand every code path that touches it without manually chaining
xrefs_to calls. Do not use for call graph traversal — use callgraph for that.
max_depth controls how many hops to follow (default 5, max 20).

tools.name: server_health
tools.docstring: Health/ready probe for MCP server and current IDB state.

tools.name: lookup_funcs
tools.docstring: Get functions by address or name (auto-detects)

tools.name: int_convert
tools.docstring: Convert numbers to different formats

tools.name: list_funcs
tools.docstring: List functions with optional filtering and offset/count pagination.

tools.name: func_query
tools.docstring: Query functions with richer filtering than list_funcs.

tools.name: list_globals
tools.docstring: List globals with optional filtering and offset/count pagination.

tools.name: entity_query
tools.docstring: Query IDB entities with typed filters, projection, and pagination.

tools.name: imports
tools.docstring: List imports with module names using offset/count pagination.

tools.name: imports_query
tools.docstring: Query imports with richer filtering than imports(offset,count).

tools.name: idb_save
tools.docstring:
Save active IDB to disk, optionally to a provided path.

In the GUI (idaq) the open database is backed by loose working files
(.id0/.id1/.id2/.nam/.til) that IDA actively manages; packing+killing them
out from under the running GUI corrupts the database on the next reopen
(issue #446). So in GUI mode this uses IDA's native in-place save
(equivalent to Ctrl+W / save_database(None, 0)), and for an explicit
different destination writes a compressed copy WITHOUT killing the live
working files.

Only headless idalib — which has no live loose files to clobber — packs into
a single compressed .i64/.idb, removing the loose working files.

tools.name: find_regex
tools.docstring: Search strings by case-insensitive regex with offset/limit pagination.

tools.name: search_text
tools.docstring:
Search the rendered listing for `pattern` over [start, end).

Iterates `idautils.Heads()` in pure Python and renders each via
`ida_lines.generate_disassembly()`. Per-head iteration is cheap and
yields between heads, so the per-tool deadline (sync_wrapper) and the
UI Cancel button both interrupt the walk reliably — unlike the C-level
`ida_search.find_text()` it replaced, which on huge .text segments
could run for minutes without polling `user_cancelled()`.

Use `start`/`end` to scope the work for predictable performance on
large binaries; without them the scan covers the whole image.

tools.name: dbg_start
tools.docstring:
Start debugger session for current target.

Requires the user to have selected a debugger (Debugger -> Select debugger)
and configured the target (executable path, arguments, attach process,
remote host, etc.). If this call fails, do not retry repeatedly. Stop,
explain to the user that debugging is not yet configured, and ask them
to set up the debugger and dismiss any IDA dialogs (e.g. "matching
executable names") before trying again.

tools.name: dbg_status
tools.docstring: Return debugger lifecycle state and current IP if suspended.

tools.name: dbg_exit
tools.docstring: Terminate active debugger session.

tools.name: dbg_continue
tools.docstring: Resume execution in active debugger session.

tools.name: dbg_run_to
tools.docstring: Run debuggee until target address is reached.

tools.name: dbg_step_into
tools.docstring: Execute one instruction, stepping into calls.

tools.name: dbg_step_over
tools.docstring: Execute one instruction, stepping over calls.

tools.name: dbg_bps
tools.docstring: List breakpoints with address, enabled status, condition, and language.

tools.name: dbg_add_bp
tools.docstring: Add breakpoints at one or more addresses.

tools.name: dbg_delete_bp
tools.docstring: Delete breakpoints at one or more addresses.

tools.name: dbg_toggle_bp
tools.docstring: Enable or disable existing breakpoints in batch.

tools.name: dbg_set_bp_condition
tools.docstring: Set or clear breakpoint conditions in batch.

tools.name: dbg_regs_all
tools.docstring: Return full register sets for all debugger threads.

tools.name: dbg_regs_remote
tools.docstring: Return full register sets for specified thread IDs.

tools.name: dbg_regs
tools.docstring: Return full registers for current debugger thread.

tools.name: dbg_gpregs_remote
tools.docstring: Get GP registers for threads

tools.name: dbg_gpregs
tools.docstring: Get current thread GP registers

tools.name: dbg_regs_named_remote
tools.docstring: Return selected registers for a specific thread ID.

tools.name: dbg_regs_named
tools.docstring: Get specific current thread registers

tools.name: dbg_stacktrace
tools.docstring: Return current call stack with module and symbol context.

tools.name: dbg_read
tools.docstring: Read debuggee memory from one or more regions.

tools.name: dbg_write
tools.docstring: Write bytes to debuggee memory regions.

tools.name: get_bytes
tools.docstring: Read bytes from memory addresses

tools.name: get_int
tools.docstring: Read integer values from memory addresses

tools.name: get_string
tools.docstring: Read strings from memory addresses

tools.name: get_global_value
tools.docstring: Read global variable values by address or symbol name.

tools.name: patch
tools.docstring: Patch bytes at memory addresses with hex data

tools.name: put_int
tools.docstring: Write integer values to memory addresses

tools.name: add_bookmark
tools.docstring: Add or replace the IDA bookmark at an address. Set prefix="" for no prefix.

tools.name: set_comments
tools.docstring: Set comments at addresses (both disassembly and decompiler views)

tools.name: append_comments
tools.docstring: Append comments at addresses, deduping exact text by default.

tools.name: patch_asm
tools.docstring: Patch assembly instructions at addresses

tools.name: rename
tools.docstring: Batch-rename funcs/globals/locals/stack vars with dry-run options.

tools.name: define_func
tools.docstring: Define functions; IDA infers bounds unless end is provided.

tools.name: define_code
tools.docstring: Convert bytes to code instruction(s) at address(es).

tools.name: undefine
tools.docstring: Undefine item(s) at address(es), converting back to raw bytes.

tools.name: force_recompile
tools.docstring:
Invalidate the Hex-Rays decompile cache for one or more functions.

Use after `set_type`, `rename` (especially of locals), `set_op_type`, or
`make_data` so the next `decompile` call regenerates fresh pseudocode
instead of returning a cached, stale view.

tools.name: set_op_type
tools.docstring:
Set the type of an instruction operand. GUI 'Y' / 'O' / '#' equivalent.

Tags an operand at a specific instruction with a desired interpretation.
Useful when the decompiler picks an awkward expression form (e.g., the
"earlier-named-symbol + offset" form for contiguous globals).

`kind` values:
- `"stroff"`: struct-offset reference. Requires `struct`, optional `delta`.
- `"offset"`: absolute offset / pointer. Optional `target_addr`.
- `"hex" | "dec" | "char" | "binary" | "octal"`: numeric format.
- `"stkvar"`: stack-variable reference (function-local).

tools.name: make_data
tools.docstring:
Create a typed data symbol at an address, replacing any prior items.

Use this to harden a symbol boundary that the decompiler is currently
expressing through a neighboring global plus offset. `set_type` alone
leaves the underlying byte items unchanged; this tool deletes them
first, then re-creates them at the right size with the right type, then
optionally renames.

The `type` field is a full C declaration with a placeholder name, e.g.
`"ISP_SNS_STATE_S * g_pastImx290[4]"` — IDA parses it via SetType.

tools.name: py_eval
tools.docstring: Execute Python in IDA context and return result/stdout/stderr.

tools.name: py_exec_file
tools.docstring:
Execute a Python script file in IDA context and return stdout/stderr.

Unlike py_eval, this runs the entire file with exec() using a single shared
globals dict (no locals split), so top-level definitions are visible to all
code in the script. Handles large scripts that would be unwieldy as inline code.

tools.name: make_signature
tools.docstring:
Create unique byte signatures for addresses. Generates the shortest
unique signature starting at each address by walking instructions and
wildcarding operands. Useful for finding stable patterns that survive
recompilation.

tools.name: make_signature_for_function
tools.docstring:
Create unique byte signatures for function entry points. Resolves each
name/address to a function, then generates the shortest unique signature
starting at the function start.

tools.name: make_signature_for_range
tools.docstring:
Create a byte signature for a specific address range (e.g. a selected
region). Unlike make_signature, this does NOT guarantee uniqueness — it
simply encodes the bytes in the range with optional operand wildcarding.

tools.name: find_xref_signatures
tools.docstring:
Find signatures for code locations that reference an address. For each
input address, finds all code cross-references TO it, generates a unique
signature at each xref site, and returns the shortest ones. Ideal for
creating signatures for data addresses, vtable entries, or string
references that can't be signatured directly.

tools.name: stack_frame
tools.docstring: Return stack variables for function address(es).

tools.name: declare_stack
tools.docstring: Create stack variables from typed stack declarations.

tools.name: delete_stack
tools.docstring: Delete stack variables by name or offset.

tools.name: survey_binary
tools.docstring:
Get a compact overview of the binary in one call. Returns file metadata,
segment layout, entry points, statistics, top 15 strings and functions ranked
by xref count (functions include classification: thunk/wrapper/leaf/dispatcher/
complex), imports by category, and call graph summary. Use this as your FIRST
tool call when starting analysis. Do not call list_funcs, imports, or find_regex
separately for triage — this returns all of that. Use detail_level='minimal'
for binaries with >10k functions.

tools.name: declare_type
tools.docstring: Declare C type definitions in local type library.

tools.name: enum_upsert
tools.docstring: Create or extend local enums in an idempotent way.

tools.name: read_struct
tools.docstring: Read struct fields from memory at address; auto-detect type when possible.

tools.name: search_structs
tools.docstring: Search local structs/unions by name pattern.

tools.name: type_query
tools.docstring: Query local types with structured filters/projection-friendly output.

tools.name: type_inspect
tools.docstring: Inspect named types (size/kind/declaration/members).

tools.name: set_type
tools.docstring: Apply types (function/global/local/stack)

tools.name: type_apply_batch
tools.docstring: Apply multiple type edits and return aggregate status.

tools.name: infer_types
tools.docstring: Infer and apply likely types at target addresses.
